title: Inversion with negative adverbials
explanation:
Inversion with negative adverbials

After negative or restrictive adverbials at the start of a sentence, we use inversion (auxiliary before subject), like in questions.

  • Never have I seen such a crowd.
  • Not only did she apologise, but she also offered compensation.
  • Hardly had we arrived when the meeting started.

questions.text: [*Never have I|I have never] felt so embarrassed in a meeting.
questions.feedback: Inversion after Never at the start.

questions.text: [*Not only did|Not only] he win the prize, but he also broke the record.
[*Hardly had|Hardly] the train left when it began to rain.
[*Rarely do|Rarely] people admit their mistakes so openly.
[*Only then did|Only then] she realise how serious the problem was.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Not until the report was published ____ the public understand the scale of the crisis. | *did | do | had
questions.feedback: Not until triggers inversion in the main clause.

questions.text: Seldom ____ such a talented group of musicians perform together. | *do | does | did
Little ____ that their decision would change everything. | *did they know | they knew | they did know
No sooner ____ the door than the phone rang. | *had he closed | he had closed | did he close
questions.feedback: No sooner had + subject + past participle.

questions.text: [*Under no circumstances should|Under no circumstances should not] employees share passwords.
[*Not once did|Not once] the speaker mention the budget.
[*Only by working together can|Only by working together we can] we solve this problem.
[*Never before has|Never before have] the company received so many complaints.
[*Hardly ever do|Hardly ever] politicians answer the question directly.
